messages.author: IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
messages.text: Now that the Xiao WIO v1.1 radio is more popular, might we consider adding the NRF - WIOv1.1 to the online web flasher? I know of a few people whi have bought them from PiHut and experienced the same failures to program due to the swapped pins from the v1.0 WIO. While I have got around that by editing the code and re-compiling, talking others through the process who don't get involved in VS-Code / Platformio isn't the easiest thing to get right. (edited)
messages.content_html: Now that the Xiao WIO v1.1 radio is more popular, might we consider adding the NRF - WIOv1.1 to the online web flasher? I know of a few people whi have bought them from PiHut and experienced the same failures to program due to the swapped pins from the v1.0 WIO. While I have got around that by editing the code and re-compiling, talking others through the process who don't get involved in VS-Code / Platformio isn't the easiest thing to get right. (edited)

messages.author: IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
messages.text: I only have seen the v1.1 in the form I have, I don't know if it is also available without that connector. The issue is that when soldering to an NRF Xiao, the order of the DIP pins along the sides is different to the v1.0

After flashing with the Seeed Studio Xiao S3 WIO companion firmware, the device can pair with BT, but I get the error, "failed fetch device info" when I try to connect. Is the only way to get this package working to compile from source?

messages.author: n7njo
messages.text: Yeah, I removed previous pairings from both laptop and phone. Tried to use Liam's web client tool along with Android meshcore app. Flashed meshtastic back on the device to confirm hardware still works fine, and it does. Reverted back to meshcore, installed meshcore-cli gave Bluetooth permissions to the terminal (Mac laptop), and had a similar experience - was able to find Bluetooth device but got an error while querying device.
messages.content_html: Yeah, I removed previous pairings from both laptop and phone. Tried to use Liam's web client tool along with Android meshcore app. Flashed meshtastic back on the device to confirm hardware still works fine, and it does. Reverted back to meshcore, installed meshcore-cli gave Bluetooth permissions to the terminal (Mac laptop), and had a similar experience - was able to find Bluetooth device but got an error while querying device.

messages.author: n7njo
messages.text: I wonder if I have faulty hardware. I unplugged the working companion to connect my 2nd S3 and configure as a repeater. After multiple failed attempts of trying to flash different firmwares (MC & MT), I finally got MC flashed as a repeater. I switched back to my original companion running with the unifiedBLE, but I'm no longer able to see the device over BT in order to pair/connect consistently, if at all. If I try to use meshcore-cli on the original companion I get an error: [Wire.cpp:499] requestFrom(): i2cWriteReadNonStop ret========= CLI Rescue ========= I have a heltec v4 coming next week, maybe I'll have better luck. These ESP32 S3 where ordered when they were first released but sat unopened for over a year.
messages.content_html: I wonder if I have faulty hardware. I unplugged the working companion to connect my 2nd S3 and configure as a repeater. After multiple failed attempts of trying to flash different firmwares (MC & MT), I finally got MC flashed as a repeater. I switched back to my original companion running with the unifiedBLE, but I'm no longer able to see the device over BT in order to pair/connect consistently, if at all. If I try to use meshcore-cli on the original companion I get an error: [Wire.cpp:499] requestFrom(): i2cWriteReadNonStop ret========= CLI Rescue ========= I have a heltec v4 coming next week, maybe I'll have better luck. These ESP32 S3 where ordered when they were first released but sat unopened for over a year.

messages.text: if you hold the user button for 8 seconds while booting, it will switch to rescue mode
messages.content_html: if you hold the user button for 8 seconds while booting, it will switch to rescue mode

messages.author: n7njo
messages.text: The good news is that I now have a companion and repeater talking to each other after reflashing the repeater, using both the android app and meshcore-cli and seem to have stability even after powering off and on. It didn't appear that the case was forcing down the user button, however, I'm not going to rule that out as I had them in and out of the case to press the boot button. Unfortunately, I can't put my finger on a single thing that fixed the problem. Could it be that without a screen on the device I was unaware of the state and I was powering the device off before it had created the keys... was it changing the USB cable, was the loose plastic in the case pressing the user button... sometimes I'd check erase when flashing and sometimes not...etc. I'm not looking for an answer, just closing the loop for someone in the future ending up here.
messages.content_html: The good news is that I now have a companion and repeater talking to each other after reflashing the repeater, using both the android app and meshcore-cli and seem to have stability even after powering off and on. It didn't appear that the case was forcing down the user button, however, I'm not going to rule that out as I had them in and out of the case to press the boot button. Unfortunately, I can't put my finger on a single thing that fixed the problem. Could it be that without a screen on the device I was unaware of the state and I was powering the device off before it had created the keys... was it changing the USB cable, was the loose plastic in the case pressing the user button... sometimes I'd check erase when flashing and sometimes not...etc. I'm not looking for an answer, just closing the loop for someone in the future ending up here.
messages.reactions_html: 2
